review_scope: double nuclear envelope, inner membrane, and perinuclear lumen only
approved_next_scope: nuclear pore seat and pore-complex design may begin as a separate gated phase; full nucleus Phase 3 remains blocked

score_evidence.notes: Score is a scoped manual review score, not an automated image-similarity score. It evaluates envelope-only recognizability, layer readability, organic material treatment, and avoidance of geometric placeholder artifacts.

passed_checks: Reference image and 3D candidate are present in the same Blender scene.
Outer membrane, inner membrane, and perinuclear lumen are visually separate.
The lumen is visible as a continuous glowing layer between the two membranes.
The envelope surface has nonuniform contour and procedural organic material variation.
No pore complexes, pore gate cores, lamina, chromatin, nucleolus, or transport cargo were built in this corrective phase.
The square patch artifacts from the first corrective render pass were removed before review.
minor_issues: The candidate intentionally lacks pore complexes, so it cannot yet fully match the reference image's outer surface detail.
The board render favors envelope/lumen readability over fitting the entire reference paragraph at maximum legibility.
The cutaway edge is still cleaner and more circular than the biological reference; the next pass should break the rim with pore seats and local membrane deformation.
required_fixes_before_full_nucleus_phase: Add nuclear pore seats and pore complexes in a separate gated phase using reference_images/nucleus_specs/02_nuclear_pores.png.
Do not use torus/cylinder/sphere primitives as final pore anatomy without substantial custom deformation and review.
Run another same-scene Blender board review before adding lamina, nucleoplasm, chromatin, or nucleolus.
decision_notes: Phase 02R resolves the immediate process failure by restarting with one scoped envelope part, generating a Blender scene, showing the reference and candidate together, and writing this review only after inspecting the rendered outputs. This is not approval for full nucleus Phase 3.
